Metadata.Coram: Kannan Ramesh J, Roger Giles IJ, Anselmo Reyes IJ

Party_Details.Name: DyStar Global Holdings (Singapore) Pte Ltd
Counsel: Kannan Ramesh Roger Giles Anselmo Reyes Judge International Judge International Judge 152 Version No 1: 27 Oct 2020 (22:40 hrs) DyStar Global Holdings (Singapore) Pte Ltd v Kiri Industries Ltd [2018] SGHC(I) 06 See Chern Yang

Facts.Text: DyStar Global Holdings (Singapore) Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore as Kiri Holding Singapore Private Limited
DyStar entered into the Share Subscription and Shareholders Agreement on 2010-01-31 with Kiri Industries Limited, Kiri International (Mauritius) Private Limited, and Well Prospering Limited
DyStar issued a Convertible Bond to WPL, a subsidiary of Longsheng
Senda converted the debt under the Convertible Bond into equity, becoming the majority shareholder of DyStar
Presentation slides prepared for the April 2014 DyStar Board meeting disclosed that DyStar and DSPL had extended loans to Longsheng-related entities

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the management of a joint venture company can be conducted in a manner that unfairly prejudices the interests of a minority shareholder
Party_Details.Rule: A company must be managed in accordance with its constitutional documents and in a manner that does not unfairly prejudice minority shareholders
Party_Details.Application: The Defendants engaged in transactions that were not in the company's best interest, while all actions were commercially justified
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that certain actions by the Defendants were not in the best interest of DyStar and unfairly prejudiced Kiri as a minority shareholder.

Metadata.Coram: Kannan Ramesh J, Roger Giles IJ, Anselmo Reyes IJ

Party_Details.Name: Kiri Industries Limited

Facts.Text: Kiri Industries Limited was incorporated in India
Kiri entered into a joint venture agreement with WPL to establish Lonsen Kiri Chemical Industries Limited
Kiri, through Manish, entered into an exclusivity agreement for the proposed acquisition of Pre-Acquisition DyStar
Kiri executed a non-binding letter of intent with the State General Reserve Fund of Oman to negotiate the sale of its DyStar shares
Kiri requested that DyStar declare a dividend after DyStar reported a profit after tax of over US$100 million in 2014

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a minority shareholder can claim unfair prejudice due to the majority shareholder's control over company decisions
Party_Details.Rule: Minority shareholders are entitled to protection against actions by majority shareholders that are unfairly prejudicial to their interests
Party_Details.Application: The majority shareholder's actions were commercially justified, while these actions were prejudicial
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that while some actions were commercially justified, others were prejudicial to Kiri's interests as a minority shareholder.

Metadata.Coram: Kannan Ramesh J, Roger Giles IJ, Anselmo Reyes IJ

Party_Details.Name: Senda International Capital Limited

Facts.Text: Senda International Capital Limited was incorporated in Hong Kong and was a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zhejiang Longsheng Group Co., Ltd
Senda converted the debt under the Convertible Bond into equity, becoming the majority shareholder of DyStar
At the April 2014 Meeting, the Related Party Loans totaling US$33.5 million were disclosed to the DyStar Board

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a majority shareholder can enforce management decisions that are contested by minority shareholders
Party_Details.Rule: Majority shareholders have the right to make management decisions, provided they do not unfairly prejudice minority shareholders
Party_Details.Application: Their management decisions were in the best interest of the company, while the Defendants by counterclaim contested these decisions as prejudicial
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that while the majority shareholder's decisions were within their rights, they were not always in the best interest of the company.

Metadata.Coram: Kannan Ramesh J, Roger Giles IJ, Anselmo Reyes IJ

Party_Details.Name: Kiri Industries Limited

Facts.Text: Kiri Industries Limited was incorporated in India
Kiri entered into a joint venture agreement with WPL to establish Lonsen Kiri Chemical Industries Limited
Kiri, through Manish, entered into an exclusivity agreement for the proposed acquisition of Pre-Acquisition DyStar
Kiri executed a non-binding letter of intent with the State General Reserve Fund of Oman to negotiate the sale of its DyStar shares
Kiri requested DyStar to declare dividends based on its strong financial performance

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a minority shareholder can contest management decisions made by the majority shareholder
Party_Details.Rule: Minority shareholders can contest management decisions if they are unfairly prejudicial to their interests
Party_Details.Application: The management decisions were prejudicial to their interests, while they were justified
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that while some management decisions were justified, others were prejudicial to the minority shareholder's interests.
